calculation_note: U-value for a single homogeneous insulation layer = thermal_conductivity (W/mK) / thickness (m). Example: 100mm PIR (0.022 W/mK) => U = 0.022 / 0.1 = 0.22 W/m²K

materials.name: PIR (Polyisocyanurate) board
materials.notes: High performance rigid foam board commonly used in roofs and walls.

materials.name: Rock wool (Mineral wool)
materials.notes: Non-combustible, good acoustic performance, used in pitched roofs, walls and floors.

materials.name: Glass wool
materials.notes: Common batt insulation for cavities and lofts.

materials.name: Wood fibre
materials.notes: Breathable, natural fibre used in breathable build-ups and timber-frame insulation.

materials.name: EPS (Expanded Polystyrene)
materials.notes: Rigid foam used externally and under screeds; good compressive properties.

materials.name: XPS (Extruded Polystyrene)
materials.notes: Moisture resistant rigid foam for below-ground and insulated floors.

materials.name: Cellulose (recycled paper)
materials.notes: Blown or loose-fill insulation with good environmental credentials.

materials.name: Stud (softwood timber)
materials.notes: Typical timber stud used in framed walls; higher conductivity than insulation so affects cavity U-values where timber paths exist.

materials.name: Joist (softwood timber)
materials.notes: Typical softwood floor/roof joist. Higher conductivity than insulation; represents linear/area thermal bridge — use actual joist depth for R calculations.

materials.name: External blockwork (dense concrete block)
materials.notes: Typical dense concrete block used in external walls; relatively high thermal conductivity compared with insulation materials.

materials.name: Plasterboard (gypsum)
materials.notes: Common internal lining board; thin layers contribute modestly to thermal resistance.

materials.name: Plywood
materials.notes: Wood panel material used for sheathing and finishes; thermal conductivity similar to other timber products.

materials.name: Roof tile (clay/ceramic)
materials.notes: Clay or concrete roof tile — heavy, relatively high conductivity; modelled as an external, discontinuous layer.

materials.name: Roofing felt / bituminous membrane
materials.notes: Underlay membrane beneath tiles or slates; thin layer with modest conductivity.

materials.name: Membrane - Vapour control layer (standard taped)
materials.notes: Standard polyethylene VCL with taped laps, commonly used on warm side of insulation to control vapour and air movement.

materials.name: Membrane - Intelligent airtight membrane
materials.notes: Variable permeability airtight membrane used to tighten timber-frame and retrofit envelopes.

materials.name: Membrane - Breather membrane
materials.notes: External breather layer; improves airtightness while allowing vapour to diffuse outwards.

materials.name: Window - Single glazing
materials.notes: Traditional single-glazed window; very high heat loss compared with modern glazing.

materials.name: Window - Double glazing (<1995)
materials.notes: Older double glazing with narrow air gap and simple sealed units; higher U-value than modern sealed units with low-e coatings.

materials.name: Window - Double glazing (>1995)
materials.notes: Modern double glazing with low-e coating and argon fill; typical for retrofit and new builds.

materials.name: Window - Triple glazing
materials.notes: Triple glazed units with multiple low-e coatings and inert gas fills; best thermal performance for windows.
